audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel / Shemuel Aleph chapter 27, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for David's fear-based asylum decision, six hundred men and households, Achish and Ziklag, duration variant, southern population identities, total killing, spoil, false raid reports, witness elimination, and Achish's manipulated trust.

verses.restored: David said to his heart, Now I shall be swept away one day by hand of Saul. There is nothing good for me except that escaping, I escape to land of Philistines. Saul will despair of seeking me again in all territory of Israel, and I shall escape from his hand.
David arose and crossed over, he and six hundred men who were with him, to Achish son of Maoch, king of Gath.
David stayed with Achish at Gath, he and his men, each man and his household, David and his two wives, Ahinoam the Jezreelite and Abigail wife of Nabal the Carmelite.
It was told to Saul that David had fled to Gath, and he no longer sought him again.
David said to Achish, If please I have found favor in your eyes, let them give me a place in one of towns of field/country, and I will stay there. Why should your servant stay in royal city with you?
Achish gave him Ziklag on that day. Therefore Ziklag has belonged to kings of Judah until this day.
Number of days that David stayed in field/country of Philistines was days and four months/a year and four months.
David and his men went up and raided Geshurite, Girzite, and Amalekite communities, because they were inhabitants of land from ancient time, toward Shur and as far as land of Egypt.
David struck land and did not keep alive man or woman. He took sheep, cattle, donkeys, camels, and garments, and returned and came to Achish.
Achish said, Where did you raid today? David said, Against Negev of Judah, against Negev of Jerahmeelites, and against Negev of Kenites.
David did not keep alive man or woman to bring to Gath, saying, Lest they tell against us, saying, Thus David did. Such was his practice all days he stayed in field/country of Philistines.
Achish trusted David, saying, He has made himself utterly odious among his people Israel, and he will be my servant forever.

translation_notes: David's fear of Saul is grounded in sustained lethal pursuit, but victimization by Saul does not excuse David's later violence against other communities.
Philistine territory functions as asylum for David and his households. Refuge across enemy lines complicates ethnic binaries.
Verses 8-11 report repeated massacres of men and women and seizure of animals and goods. They must not be sanitized as ordinary raids or justified as necessary cover.
David's lie to Achish is protected by killing all potential witnesses. Strategic deception under persecution is ethically distinct from exterminating communities to conceal profitable violence.
The identities and geography of Geshurites, Girzites, and Amalekites require textual and historical audit. No modern community may be mapped onto these targets.
Achish's trust arises from manipulated information. Political asylum, patronage, and intelligence relationships require accountability on all sides.
Ahinoam, Abigail, the other families, and displaced dependents bear the risk of David's decisions and must not disappear beneath his leadership story.
safety_note: Maximum safeguards: Do not romanticize David's raids, witness-killing, plunder, or deception. Being persecuted does not confer innocence in harm done to third parties. Do not identify any living ethnic group as these ancient targets or use refugee status to stigmatize displaced people as deceptive or violent.
